results.title: GitHub - HazAT/glimpse: Native micro Web UI for scripts and agents — super fast OS native webview with bidirectional JSON communication for agents · GitHub
results.content:
`win.getInfo()`
`info`

`win.loadFile(path)` — Load a local HTML file by absolute path.

`win.loadFile(path)`

`win.show(options?)` — Reveal a hidden window. Optional `options.title` sets the window title.

`win.show(options?)`
`options.title`

`win.close()` — Close the window.

`win.close()`

### JavaScript Bridge (in-page)

Every page loaded by Glimpse gets a `window.glimpse` object injected at document start:

`window.glimpse`

## Protocol

Glimpse uses a newline-delimited JSON (JSON Lines) protocol over stdin/stdout. Each line is a complete JSON object. Any language that can spawn a process and pipe JSON can use Glimpse directly.

### Stdin → Glimpse (commands)

Set HTML — Replace page content. HTML must be base64-encoded.

Eval JavaScript — Run JS in the WebView. [...] Eval JavaScript — Run JS in the WebView.

Follow Cursor — Toggle cursor tracking. Optional `anchor` and `mode`.

`anchor`
`mode`

Load File — Load a local HTML file by absolute path.

Get Info — Request current system info. Responds with an `info` event.

`info`

Show — Reveal a hidden window. Optional `title`.

`title`

Title — Update menu bar text (status item mode only).

Resize — Change popover dimensions (status item mode only).

Close — Close the window and exit.

### Stdout → Host (events)

Ready — WebView finished loading. Includes system info.

Info — Response to `get-info`. Same shape as `ready`.

`get-info`
`ready`

Message — Data sent from the page via `window.glimpse.send(...)`.

`window.glimpse.send(...)`

Click — Menu bar icon clicked (status item mode only). [...] | Scenario | Time |
 --- |
| Warm start (subsequent runs) | ~310ms |
| First run after idle | ~630ms |
| Cold start (compile + run) | ~2,000ms |

Cold start only happens once during `npm install`. After that, it's always warm.

`npm install`

## Architecture

`src/glimpse.swift — macOS native binary (Swift/Cocoa/WebKit)
src/linux/ — Linux native binary (Rust/GTK4/WebKitGTK)
src/chromium-backend.mjs — Linux Chromium CDP backend (zero-compile alternative)
native/windows/Program.cs — Windows native binary (.NET 8/WebView2)
src/glimpse.mjs — Node.js wrapper (EventEmitter API)
src/follow-cursor-support.mjs — Runtime capability detection
bin/glimpse.mjs — CLI entry point (npx glimpseui)
scripts/build.mjs — Unified cross-platform build
scripts/postinstall.mjs — Platform-aware postinstall`

results.title: 7 Top Glimpse Trends Alternatives (2026) - Exploding Topics
results.content:
When you locate a trend in our database, you can see:

undefined

undefined

What really makes Exploding Topics stand apart from Glimpse and its other competitors, though, is how quickly we can identify trending topics. We do this through a mix of AI tools and expert human data analysis.

This combination allows us to find trends early, analyze their importance, and update our database every day.

Besides, Exploding Topics gives you a wide range of additional data for e-commerce product trends.

Unlike Glimpse, Exploding Topics has dedicated Trending Products report that tracks standard metrics like search volume and trend growth, and also shows Amazon sales, revenue, and review data.

trending-beauty-products.webp

trending-beauty-products.webp [...] ### 4. JungleScout

JungleScout is an Amazon sales optimization platform that includes a product trend spotting tool and a Chrome extension.

undefined

undefined

If you're interested in Google Trends Supercharged for Amazon e-commerce purposes, then JungleScout may be a better option.

The tool allows you to see:

If you sell on Amazon, this data can help you:

JungleScout only provides data about Amazon product trends, though. If you're selling on another e-commerce platform, you'll be better served by another tool on this list.

#### How Much Does JungleScout Cost?

There isn't a free JungleScout plan. To use the service, you'll need to opt in to a subscription starting at $29 per month.

#### Glimpse vs. JungleScout: At a Glance [...] #### Glimpse vs. JungleScout: At a Glance

|  |  |  |
 --- 
|  | Glimpse | JungleScout |
| Starting price | $99/mo | $29/mo |
| Chrome extension | Yes | Yes |
| Special features | See extra data while browsing Google Trends | Daily and historical Amazon sales trend information |
| Useful for | Heavy Google Trends users | Amazon FBA sellers |

### 5. Trends.vc

Trends.vc is a trend-spotting community.

undefined

undefined

When using Trends.vc, you can explore topics related to crypto, AI, and more.

Community members get access to:

The entire Trends.vc platform has a definite entrepreneurial and investment focus. If you want to explore trends while building a business, then you may enjoy the community-focused aspect of Trends.vc.
